label: fiction
title: The Purple Label
setting: A meal-preparation contamination trial in 2026, where a personal grudge competes with a packaging printer that reused yesterday’s allergen label.
charge: Intentionally contaminating prepared food and causing serious injury
elements: The accused performed or participated in the alleged act.
The act was deliberate or dishonest rather than accidental.
The prosecution has proved every required fact beyond reasonable doubt.
hook: The purple label promises safety. Inside the sealed meal is the one ingredient the customer cannot survive.

accused.human: Rina Dovel runs free weekend cooking classes and supports a child with severe allergies at home.
accused.if_guilty: A conviction means prison, loss of food-safety work, and public branding as someone who weaponised the danger she understands personally.

prosecution.speaker: pc
prosecution.text: The prosecution says the accused threatened a former friend, selected the dangerous ingredient, and signed the batch that sent the meal out. The apparent centre of the case is an angry message sent by the accused, but the evidence must be followed through the complete sequence rather than treated as a single dramatic clue.

defence.speaker: dc
defence.text: The defence says the threat was private anger while a documented printer fault placed a safe label on the wrong meal during a rushed shift. Suspicion gathered around a person before investigators tested the limits of an angry message sent by the accused; those limits matter because a criminal verdict cannot rest on an attractive shortcut.

prosecution.speaker: pc
prosecution.text: The separate records now meet at the same point. a printer queue that reused the previous day’s label does not depend on rumour or hindsight, and it fits the conduct before and after the event. Taken together, the prosecution says the elements are proved beyond reasonable doubt.

defence.speaker: dc
defence.text: The prosecution still asks you to bridge uncertainty with suspicion. an angry message sent by the accused looked powerful at first, yet every witness accepted limits around it. If the remaining record supports a reasonable innocent account, your duty is to return not guilty.

epilogue: The injured customer recovered slowly and accepted Rina’s apology for the message, not for a crime. The kitchen introduced scan-to-pack checks, and Rina returned only after completing additional safety training beside the worker who found the printer fault. The people involved kept living with the consequences after the courtroom emptied. The case changed workplace rules, but it could not return the months spent under accusation.

beats.speaker: pc
beats.text: The sealed meal contained ground nut paste while its purple label declared the batch free from that ingredient. Rina signed the shift sheet covering packing and dispatch. The record was created at the time and its origin is not disputed. The jury saw the complete item, including the limits written beside the result. Nothing in that account depends on a later reconstruction of events.
beats.reveal_note: The model scored correlation and everyone downstream read causation. She worked the busiest shifts at the registers where shrinkage happens — so, therefore, did the shrinkage. Eighty-seven measured her roster, not her honesty.

beats.speaker: w2
beats.text: Rina and I had ended a friendship badly. She messaged that I would regret humiliating her, then my throat closed after three bites of a meal carrying the safe label. I recorded that detail before anyone suggested what my evidence should mean. I remember it because it was unusual, and I have not changed my account.
beats.reveal_note: He watched a service-desk supervisor doing a service-desk job — hovering, voiding, assisting — with a number that said thief propped beside the screen. The score wrote the story; the footage just illustrated it.

beats.speaker: w1
beats.text: You initially called the wrong label deliberate? Yes, before the other three meals were found. Those meals showed both kinds of mismatch? They did, which was not consistent with targeting one customer. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: You initially called the wrong label deliberate?

turns.speaker: w1
turns.text: Yes, before the other three meals were found.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: Those meals showed both kinds of mismatch?

turns.speaker: w1
turns.text: They did, which was not consistent with targeting one customer. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today.

beats.reveal_note: A quarter of surveillance produced no theft, no pocketed note, no bag check failure — only a number, restated with increasing confidence.

beats.speaker: pc
beats.text: The kitchen camera shows Rina placing ingredient tubs near the line. It does not show her opening this meal or changing a label after the tray was sealed. The record was created at the time and its origin is not disputed. The jury saw the complete item, including the limits written beside the result. Nothing in that account depends on a later reconstruction of events.
beats.reveal_note: Service-desk staff process the whole store's refunds — the void count was her job description rendered as statistics. The no-sales were locker change and trolley coins, required by the same head office now prosecuting them.

beats.speaker: dc
beats.text: The printer lost its network connection at 11:42. When that model reconnects, it can repeat the last cached label unless the operator clears the local queue. The record was created at the time and its origin is not disputed. The jury saw the complete item, including the limits written beside the result. Nothing in that account depends on a later reconstruction of events.
beats.reveal_note: The experiment the dashboard never thought to run: remove the suspect, watch the number. The number went up. Whatever was leaking, it was not her.

beats.speaker: w3
beats.text: Three surviving meals packed within the same minute had wrong labels: two ordinary meals marked safe and one safe meal marked as containing the ingredient. I recorded that detail before anyone suggested what my evidence should mean. I remember it because it was unusual, and I have not changed my account. That is what I personally saw, heard, or did; I cannot take it further.
beats.reveal_note: A light scale and a double-counted dock: two boring faults that manufactured a crime wave, and a model happy to pin it on whoever worked the most Saturdays.

beats.speaker: w4
beats.text: I signed the shift sheet because I supervised six lines. I sent an ugly message after an argument, but I did not touch Orin’s tray or try to hurt anyone. I recorded that detail before anyone suggested what my evidence should mean. I remember it because it was unusual, and I have not changed my account.
beats.reveal_note: She reported the actual cause of the shrinkage twice, months before the model blamed her for it. Both tickets were closed without anyone testing the scale.

beats.speaker: w4
beats.text: You believed the message referred to this meal? I did because it arrived the night before. Rina never mentioned food or an allergy? No. She said I would regret humiliating her. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today.

turns.speaker: pc
turns.text: You believed the message referred to this meal?

turns.speaker: w4
turns.text: I did because it arrived the night before.

turns.speaker: pc
turns.text: Rina never mentioned food or an allergy?

turns.speaker: w4
turns.text: No. She said I would regret humiliating her. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today.

beats.reveal_note: A tight budget shared with half the workforce, and an impossible memory test no honest cashier could pass — the cross-examination proved she was ordinary, not guilty.

beats.speaker: w2
beats.text: You knew the printer had dropped offline? I saw the warning but thought it had recovered. You did not clear the local queue? No. The manual did not tell operators to do that after reconnection. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: You knew the printer had dropped offline?

turns.speaker: w2
turns.text: I saw the warning but thought it had recovered.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: You did not clear the local queue?

turns.speaker: w2
turns.text: No. The manual did not tell operators to do that after reconnection. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today.

beats.reveal_note: It took three employees to replace the workload that the dashboard had scored as one woman's crime spree.

beats.speaker: judge
beats.text: The prosecution must prove every element beyond reasonable doubt. The accused does not have to prove an alternative account, explain every suspicious detail, or show who else was responsible. Treat digital records like any other evidence: examine their origin, limits, and connection to a person. The record was created at the time and its origin is not disputed.
beats.reveal_note: The direction the era needed: a score cannot take the oath. Somewhere between the scale and the dock, the actual facts had been waiting the whole time.

twist: Rina’s message was cruel but did not describe food. The label printer had reissued yesterday’s safe template after losing connection, and three other meals from the same minute carried mismatched ingredient labels.

jurors.label: Juror 2
jurors.persona: A quick first impression matters to this juror, but a concrete contradiction can still move them.

agree: That argument fits a recorded part of this case, and I give it real weight without treating it as infallible.

pushback: I am not ready to let one attractive clue decide The Purple Label; show me how the complete sequence fits.
I am not ready to let one attractive clue decide The Purple Label; show me how the complete sequence fits. I want the room to test that point once more.
concede: That changes how I understand an angry message sent by the accused; it is less complete than it first appeared.
final: My verdict turns on whether a printer queue that reused the previous day’s label survives the burden and the competing explanation.
